mcq.question: What is an instruction set?
mcq.correct: A list of commands a CPU can process
wrong: A section of memory used by the CPU
A system for storing program data
A method for entering data into a computer

mcq.question: In what form are instructions stored?
Why is an instruction set important?
mcq.correct: It defines what operations a CPU can perform
wrong: It determines how data is displayed
It controls how data is stored permanently

mcq.question: What happens if a CPU does not recognise an instruction?
wrong: It skips the instruction and continues
It converts it into a recognised command

mcq.question: Do all CPUs have the same instruction set?
wrong: Yes, all CPUs use the same set of instructions
Only modern CPUs have different instruction sets
Only high-performance CPUs have instruction sets
